seoTitle: How to Set a Default Slicer Value in Power BI Easily (2026)
metaDescription: Learn how to lock a Power BI slicer to a default selection using bookmarks and a reset button so dashboards open with a consistent view.
intro: Opening a dashboard to a blank or unfiltered state forces users to manually pick the current year or region every time. This guide shows how to lock a slicer to a specific default selection using Power BI's bookmarks pane, then build a reset button so users can quickly return to that baseline view. It's aimed at report developers who want a consistent starting point for every viewer.

steps.heading: Choose your default value
steps.body: Click your category or timeline slicer and select the specific value you want to establish as the baseline choice.

steps.heading: Enable single select if needed
steps.body: In the Format visual pane, expand Slicer settings, and toggle Single select on if your dashboard logic requires exactly one active value.

steps.heading: Open the Bookmarks pane
steps.body: Go to the View tab and check the box for Bookmarks pane, then click Add to capture your current filter state.

steps.heading: Rename and configure the bookmark
steps.body: Rename the entry to something like Default Slicer View, and deselect Data or Display properties as needed based on your layout.

steps.heading: Build a reset button
steps.body: Insert a Button, turn on its Action card, set the Type to Bookmark, and map it to your saved Default Slicer View so users can return to it anytime.

faq.question: Does a default slicer selection persist for every viewer?
faq.answer: Power BI caches layout selections, and a bookmark-based default combined with a reset button gives you the most reliable consistent starting view across sessions.

faq.question: Can users still change the slicer after I set a default?
faq.answer: Yes, unless you enable Single select restrictions, users can adjust the slicer freely and use your reset button to return to the default.

faq.question: What's the purpose of the reset button?
faq.answer: It lets users quickly jump back to your saved bookmark state if they've changed filters and want to return to the baseline view.

faq.question: Do I need to publish my report for the default view to work?
faq.answer: Yes, publish your file to your team workspace and verify the default view renders correctly in the web browser.
